course_title: Social Pharmacy and the Health System 3B
course_description: The purpose of this course is to examine the relationships between society and health. How do social factors influence health and healthcare? How do health and healthcare influence social organisation and participation? These questions are addressed by exploring the aims, methods and contributions of the social sciences in the context of contemporary case studies in health.

prerequisites: We recommend completing the following courses before enrolling in this one:

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
course_aims: Identify and discuss the relationship between society and health. Develop an understanding of the ways in which social factors can influence health and the quality use of medicines.

learning_outcomes.description: Describe the aims, methods and theoretical perspectives of the social sciences
Apply the theories and methods of the social sciences to analyse case studies relating to health, illness and healthcare
Explain how our history, experience, culture, beliefs and relationships can impact medication safety and effectiveness, medication adherence and health outcomes

Understand the ways in which culture informs patient centred and personalised care to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and other Australians of diverse cultural and linguistic backgrounds
Evaluate how local and global initiatives (e.g. Royal Commission findings, Sustainable Development Goals) seek to improve health outcomes and the provision of healthcare

Demonstrate thinking skills individually and within a group by identifying and evaluating arguments; recognising and considering alternative points of view, and clearly expressing and justifying a position

assessment_details.title: Engagement Tasks
assessment_details.due_date: There is an engagement task for each learning week. The task is due 3 pm the day before the relevant tutorial for your group.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: There are a series of key readings and tasks to be completed throughout the semester to demonstrate engagement with the course and attainment of learning objectives. The worksheet for each engagement task is provided on the course UQ Extend site. Each engagement task contributes up to 2 marks. There is an engagement task for each learning week. The maximum mark you can receive for this assessment is 10. You are encouraged to submit all engagement tasks, but there are a range of ways you could achieve full marks. For example, you could submit 5 engagement task that are each awarded 2 marks, or you could submit 8 engagement tasks—6 x 1 mark plus 2 x 2 marks. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. If you use software to improve your writing (e.g. Grammarly), take care not to use generative AI tools within the software. You need to be able to submit an unedited draft of your work on request. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit via the links provided in Learn.UQ (Blackboard).
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Late submission
You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .

assessment_details.title: Reflexive essay: Indigenous health
assessment_details.due_date: 31/10/2025 3:00 pm All the information to be able to complete the assessment will have been provided by Week 7. Students are encouraged to plan a time to complete the assessment that fits their schedule and submit when ready prior to the due date. See Learn.UQ (Blackboard) for details and marking criteria.
assessment_details.task_description: Reflect on your ability to promote and advocate for cultural safety, respect and responsiveness in relation to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples. This assessment expands on the work started in the Cultural Safety in Practice Modules completed in year 1 and 2. See Learn.UQ (Blackboard) for details and marking criteria. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or machine translation technologies, successful completion of this assessment will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit via the link provided in Learn.UQ (Blackboard).
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

The stimulus will come from one of the cases discussed throughout the semester. See Learn.UQ (Blackboard) for details and marking criteria. assessment_details.title: Debate
assessment_details.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Participation/ Student contribution, Presentation
assessment_details.due_date: 13/10/2025 - 31/10/2025 The debate will take place during the tutorials.
assessment_details.task_description: Engage in an in-class debate with a small group of peers on a topic in social pharmacy or society and health. You are given time in tutorials throughout the semester to work on the debate. While the task is completed within a group, your work is marked individually based on your contribution (30%). If a member of the group is unable to complete their contribution to the debate on the scheduled date, the rest of the group will proceed in their absence. Submit your debate script with an annotated bibliography by 2pm the day after your in-class debate is scheduled. The debate script and annotated bibliography is worth 10%. See Learn.UQ (Blackboard) for details and marking criteria. The debate will be recorded and retained as per university policy. The recording will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed if required for the purposes of moderation of marking; provision of feedback to the student; and/or re-marking following a successful re-mark application. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The debate is an in-class activity.
Submit your debate script and annotated bibliography via the link provided on Learn.UQ (Blackboard).
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 21 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Students awarded an extension will need to complete the assessment at a time that will be scheduled before the end of the examination period.
Late submission
You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (28 Jul - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Tutorial Topics Students attend a two-hour tutorial in 11 weeks of semester: Tutorial 1: Health and the social sciences Tutorials 2-3: Obesity Tutorials 4-5: Opioid crisis Tutorials 6-7: Indigenous health Tutorials 8-9: Pharmacy and pharmacists in the health system Tutorials 10-11: Debate Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 11 (28 Jul - 19 Oct)
learning_activities.topic: Active online learning (UQ Extend) Topic 1: Health and the social sciences Topics 2-3: Obesity Topics 4-5: Opioid crisis Topics 6-7: Indigenous health Topics 8-9: Pharmacy and pharmacists in the health system Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
